figure.title: Rx Flit Processing Logic
figure.purpose: Details the logical flow for processing incoming Rx Flits, including CRC validation, acknowledgment, and enqueuing.

technical_summary: Divided into three main sections: Rx Ingress Rules, Rx Ack and Replay Request Processing Rules, and Rx Enqueuing Rules.
Rx Ingress handles CRC checks and replay counters.
Rx Ack Processing handles parsing Op codes ('command', 'rpy', 'Ack') and validating sequence numbers (ackReqSeq) to manage retransmission or acknowledgment.
Rx Enqueuing dictates whether a valid payload flit is added to the receive queue or dropped.

components.name: Rx Ingress Rules
components.description: Initial flit filtering based on CRC.

components.name: Rx Ack and Replay Rules
components.description: State management for handling acks and replays.

components.name: Rx Enqueuing Rules
components.description: Logic for storing data into the Rx queue.

steps: Enqueue valid payloads or trigger retransmissions.

critical_facts: The receiver uses a complex set of conditional rules to robustly handle missing or corrupted flits and coordinate with the transmitter's replay queue.
